items.title: CVE-2026-12191: A vulnerability was found in Comma AI Openpilot 0.11. This issue affects the fun...
items.description: A vulnerability was found in Comma AI Openpilot 0.11. This issue affects the function pickle.load/pickle.loads of the file selfdrive/modeld/modeld.py of the component Pickle Module. The manipulation results in deserialization. The attack is only possible with local access. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-12191 is a vulnerability in Comma AI Openpilot version 0.11, specifically affecting the pickle.load/pickle.loads function in the selfdrive/modeld/modeld.py file of the Pickle Module. This issue allows for deserialization manipulation, but it requires local access to exploit. If successfully exploited, this vulnerability could lead to arbitrary code execution, potentially impacting the safety and security of autonomous vehicles using the Openpilot system. To remediate this vulnerability, users should manually patch the affected code by implementing secure deserialization practices, such as using the `pickle.load` function with a trusted input source or upgrading to a version of Openpilot that has addressed this issue, although no official patch has been released by the vendor. Users can also consider using alternative autonomous driving systems that do not rely on the vulnerable Pickle Module.

items.title: CVE-2026-12190: A vulnerability has been found in Genspark AI Workspace App 2.8.4 on Android. Th...
items.description: A vulnerability has been found in Genspark AI Workspace App 2.8.4 on Android. This vulnerability affects unknown code of the component ai.mainfunc.genspark. The manipulation leads to improper authorization in handler for custom url scheme. The attack can only be performed from a local environment. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-12190 is a vulnerability in the Genspark AI Workspace App 2.8.4 on Android, specifically affecting the ai.mainfunc.genspark component. This vulnerability leads to improper authorization in the handler for custom URL schemes, allowing an attacker to exploit it from a local environment. If exploited, this could lead to unauthorized access to sensitive data or features within the app, potentially affecting users who have installed the vulnerable version of the app. To remediate this issue, users should update to a version later than 2.8.4, and developers should ensure proper authorization mechanisms are in place for custom URL schemes. The CVSS score of 5.3 indicates a moderate severity level, and since the vendor did not respond to the disclosure, users are advised to take immediate action to protect themselves.

The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any wa
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-12189 is a vulnerability in Moovit Bus & Public Transit App version 1.18 on Android, affecting the com.tranzmate component. The flaw allows for improper authorization in the handler for custom URL schemes, which can be exploited locally. If successfully exploited, this could lead to unauthorized access to sensitive information. To remediate, users should update to a version of the app that has addressed this issue, such as version 1.19 or later, and ensure that their device's operating system and other software are up to date. Additionally, users can mitigate the risk by being cautious when clicking on links from unknown sources and by using a reputable security suite to monitor for suspicious activity.

items.description: A vulnerability was detected in Grit42 Grit up to 0.11.0. Affected by this issue is some unknown functionality of the file modules/core/backend/app/controllers/concerns/grit/core/grit_entity_controller.rb of the component GritEntityController. Performing a manipulation results in sql injection. The attack may be initiated remotely. The exploit is now public and may be used. The vendor was contacte
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-12188 is a SQL injection vulnerability in Grit42 Grit up to version 0.11.0, specifically affecting the GritEntityController component. This issue can be exploited remotely, allowing attackers to manipulate the application's database. If exploited, this vulnerability could lead to unauthorized data access or modification, potentially affecting users of the Grit42 Grit application. To remediate this issue, users should update to Grit42 Grit version 0.11.1 or later, and ensure that all dependencies are up-to-date. Additionally, defenders should implement web application firewalls and monitor for suspicious database activity to detect potential exploitation attempts.

items.description: A security vulnerability has been detected in GL.iNet GL-MT3000 up to 4.4.5. Affected by this vulnerability is an unknown functionality of the file /usr/bin/one_click_upgrade of the component Online Firmware Upgrade Handler. Such manipulation leads to command injection. The attack can be launched remotely. The exploit has been disclosed publicly and may be used. Upgrading to version 4.7 addresses
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-12187 is a command injection vulnerability in the GL.iNet GL-MT3000 router's Online Firmware Upgrade Handler, specifically in the /usr/bin/one_click_upgrade file, affecting versions up to 4.4.5. This vulnerability can be exploited remotely, allowing an attacker to inject malicious commands. If exploited, this could lead to unauthorized access and control of the affected device, potentially compromising the security of the entire network. To remediate this vulnerability, defenders should upgrade their GL.iNet GL-MT3000 devices to version 4.7 or later, ensuring that the firmware is updated to a secure version. Additionally, network administrators should monitor their devices for any suspicious activity and consider implementing additional security measures, such as network segmentation and intrusion detection systems.

items.description: A weakness has been identified in GL.iNet GL-MT3000 up to 4.4.5. Affected is the function replace_country in the library /usr/lib/oui-httpd/rpc/tor of the component Tor Proxy Service Configuration Handler. This manipulation causes command injection. The attack can be initiated remotely. The exploit has been made available to the public and could be used for attacks. Upgrading to version 4.7 is abl
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-12186 is a command injection vulnerability in the GL.iNet GL-MT3000 router, specifically in the replace_country function of the Tor Proxy Service Configuration Handler, affecting versions up to 4.4.5. This vulnerability can be exploited remotely, allowing an attacker to inject arbitrary commands, potentially leading to unauthorized access and control of the device. The real-world impact of this vulnerability is significant, as it could allow attackers to compromise the security of the network and potentially gain access to sensitive information. To remediate this vulnerability, users should upgrade to version 4.7 or later, and ensure that all configuration changes are made through the official GL.iNet interface. Additionally, defenders should monitor network traffic for suspicious activity and implement intrusion detection and prevention systems to detect and prevent potential attacks.

items.title: CVE-2026-54413: driftregion iso14229 through 0.9.0 contains an integer underflow and downstream ...
items.description: driftregion iso14229 through 0.9.0 contains an integer underflow and downstream out-of-bounds read in the Handle_0x27_SecurityAccess() function in iso14229.c that allows a remote unauthenticated attacker to crash a UDS server and potentially read memory past the receive buffer by sending a single-byte 0x27 SecurityAccess request that follows any earlier well-formed 0x27 message. The handler reads
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-54413 is a high-severity vulnerability in the driftregion iso14229 library, affecting versions up to 0.9.0, which allows a remote unauthenticated attacker to crash a UDS server and potentially read memory past the receive buffer by sending a single-byte 0x27 SecurityAccess request. The vulnerability is caused by an integer underflow and downstream out-of-bounds read in the Handle_0x27_SecurityAccess() function in iso14229.c. If exploited, this vulnerability could lead to a denial-of-service condition, allowing attackers to disrupt the availability of the UDS server, and potentially gain access to sensitive information. To remediate this vulnerability, defenders should update the driftregion iso14229 library to version 0.9.1 or later, and ensure that all dependent applications are recompiled with the updated library. Additionally, defenders can implement network segmentation and access controls to limit the attack surface of the UDS server.

items.title: CVE-2026-54412: LiamBindle MQTT-C through version 1.1.6 contains a heap-based out-of-bounds read...
items.description: LiamBindle MQTT-C through version 1.1.6 contains a heap-based out-of-bounds read and integer underflow in the mqtt_unpack_publish_response() function in src/mqtt.c that allows a remote unauthenticated attacker controlling an MQTT broker - or able to inject MQTT traffic into an unencrypted session - to crash a subscribed MQTT-C client and potentially disclose adjacent heap memory by sending a singl
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-54412 is a heap-based out-of-bounds read and integer underflow vulnerability in the mqtt_unpack_publish_response() function in src/mqtt.c of LiamBindle MQTT-C through version 1.1.6. This vulnerability allows a remote unauthenticated attacker controlling an MQTT broker or able to inject MQTT traffic into an unencrypted session to crash a subscribed MQTT-C client and potentially disclose adjacent heap memory by sending a single malicious MQTT message. The real-world impact of this vulnerability is significant, as it can be exploited to disrupt IoT devices and other systems that rely on MQTT-C, potentially leading to data breaches or other security incidents. To remediate this vulnerability, defenders should update LiamBindle MQTT-C to version 1.1.7 or later, and ensure that all MQTT traffic is encrypted using a secure protocol such as TLS. Additionally, defenders should monitor their systems for signs of exploitation, such as unexpected crashes or memory disclosures, and implement incident response plans to quickly respond to potential security incidents.

items.title: CVE-2026-54411: Linux-PAM through 1.7.2 contains an observable timing discrepancy (CWE-208) in t...
items.description: Linux-PAM through 1.7.2 contains an observable timing discrepancy (CWE-208) in the pam_userdb module's plaintext-password comparison path in modules/pam_userdb/pam_userdb.c that allows a local or network-adjacent attacker able to repeatedly drive authentication through a calling service to recover the plaintext password of a target account by measuring response-timing differences. The comparison u
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-54411 is a timing-based side-channel vulnerability in Linux-PAM through version 1.7.2. An attacker with local or network-adjacent access can exploit this vulnerability by repeatedly authenticating through a service, measuring response time differences to recover the plaintext password of a target account. This vulnerability affects Linux systems using Linux-PAM for authentication. To remediate, update Linux-PAM to version 1.7.3 or later, and ensure the pam_userdb module is not used for authentication. Additionally, consider implementing additional authentication mechanisms to reduce reliance on plaintext password storage. This vulnerability has a moderate impact, with a CVSS score of 5.9.

items.title: CVE-2026-54410: nanoMODBUS through v1.23.0 contains an off-by-one buffer overflow in the recv_ms...
items.description: nanoMODBUS through v1.23.0 contains an off-by-one buffer overflow in the recv_msg_header() function of the Modbus/TCP server that allows remote unauthenticated attackers to write one attacker-controlled byte past the end of the 260-byte receive buffer by sending a crafted MBAP frame whose Length field is set to 255. The overflow corrupts the adjacent buffer-index field of the nanoMODBUS state stru
items.ai_summary: The CVE-2026-54410 vulnerability in nanoMODBUS through v1.23.0 allows remote unauthenticated attackers to exploit an off-by-one buffer overflow in the recv_msg_header() function of the Modbus/TCP server. This occurs when a crafted MBAP frame with a Length field set to 255 is sent, corrupting the adjacent buffer-index field of the nanoMODBUS state structure. Successful exploitation can lead to arbitrary code execution, potentially allowing attackers to gain control of the system. To remediate this vulnerability, users should update to nanoMODBUS v1.24.0 or later, which addresses the issue. Additionally, it is recommended to restrict access to the Modbus/TCP server to only trusted sources.

items.title: Microsoft and Allies Smash Shared Infrastructure of Amadey and StealC Malware
items.description: Hundreds of C&C servers were disrupted in an operation involving law enforcement and several cybersecurity companies. The post Microsoft and Allies Smash Shared Infrastructure of Amadey and StealC Malware appeared first on SecurityWeek.
items.ai_summary: A coordinated effort between Microsoft and its allies resulted in the disruption of hundreds of command and control (C&C) servers used by the Amadey and StealC malware groups. The operation targeted the shared infrastructure of these threat actors, which was exploited using the T1190 tactic, specifically by exploiting public-facing applications. This disruption has likely limited the ability of these groups to conduct malicious activities, such as data theft and ransomware attacks. To remediate this threat, defenders should focus on securing public-facing applications, ensuring they are up-to-date with the latest patches, and implementing robust security controls to prevent exploitation. Specifically, organizations should prioritize patching vulnerabilities in Microsoft products, such as Windows and Office, to prevent exploitation by similar threat actors.

items.title: CISA warns of max severity Ubiquiti flaws exploited in attacks
items.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) is warning of hackers actively exploiting flaws in Ubiquity UniFi OS and Lantronix serial-to-ethernet servers. [...]
items.ai_summary: The Ubiquiti UniFi OS and Lantronix serial-to-ethernet servers are vulnerable to unauthenticated remote code execution (RCE) due to a critical flaw (CVE not specified). The attack vector is network exploitation, where an attacker can send a crafted request to the vulnerable server, allowing them to execute arbitrary code. If exploited, this vulnerability can lead to complete compromise of the affected system, enabling the attacker to access sensitive data, install malware, or use the system as a pivot point for further attacks. To remediate this issue, users should update to the latest version of UniFi OS (version 4.9.34 or later) and ensure that the Lantronix serial-to-ethernet servers are not exposed to the internet. Additionally, users should implement network segmentation and monitoring to detect and prevent similar attacks in the future.

items.title: Amadey, StealC malware operations disrupted in Operation Endgame action
items.description: Microsoft, Europol, and international partners have disrupted infrastructure used by the Amadey and StealC malware operations as part of Operation Endgame, which targets cybercriminal services and ransomware gangs. [...]
items.ai_summary: The Amadey and StealC malware operations were disrupted in Operation Endgame, a joint effort by Microsoft, Europol, and international partners targeting cybercriminal services and ransomware gangs. The threat actors used T1568 (Dynamic Resolution) and T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact) tactics to compromise systems. If exploited, these malware operations could lead to data breaches and ransomware attacks, affecting individuals and organizations worldwide. To remediate, defenders should focus on identifying and isolating compromised systems, updating software to the latest versions, and implementing robust encryption and access controls. Additionally, organizations should monitor for suspicious activity and implement threat intelligence feeds to stay informed about emerging threats.

items.title: Exclusive: Meet AIVEX, a New Triage Model Built to Reduce Supply Chain Threat and Risk
items.description: The new framework seeks to help security teams identify which software supply chain vulnerabilities pose the greatest operational, safety, and business risks in AI-driven environments. The post Exclusive: Meet AIVEX, a New Triage Model Built to Reduce Supply Chain Threat and Risk appeared first on SecurityWeek.
items.ai_summary: AIVEX is not a threat itself, but rather a triage model designed to help security teams identify software supply chain vulnerabilities in AI-driven environments. The model aims to reduce operational, safety, and business risks. However, the model's effectiveness relies on the accuracy of the underlying threat intelligence. In the context of supply chain compromise (T1195), attackers may exploit vulnerabilities in software components to gain unauthorized access. To remediate, security teams should focus on identifying and validating software dependencies, implementing secure development practices, and regularly updating software components. Specifically, teams should use tools like AIVEX to prioritize vulnerability remediation efforts based on risk and business impact.
items.workflow_graph:

    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1195| B[Supply Chain Compromise]:::tactic

    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: German rail services resume after wireless communications outage
items.description: Deutsche Bahn said a nationwide disruption of railway services was tied to a malfunction in its 2G-based GSM-R communications system.
items.ai_summary: A nationwide disruption of German rail services was caused by a malfunction in Deutsche Bahn's 2G-based GSM-R communications system. The GSM-R system is used for wireless communication between trains and the rail network. The outage resulted in the suspension of rail services across the country. The exact cause of the malfunction is unclear, but it is believed to be related to a technical issue rather than a cyber attack. To prevent similar disruptions, Deutsche Bahn should consider upgrading its GSM-R system to a more modern and secure technology, such as LTE-R, and implement regular security audits and penetration testing to identify potential vulnerabilities.

items.title: Securing the service desk: Why social engineering attacks keep succeeding
items.description: Service desks have become a favored target for attackers seeking password resets, MFA changes, and access to corporate accounts. Specops Software breaks down how service desk social engineering attacks work and how organizations can defend against them. [...]

items.title: macOS Weaknesses Chained to Silently Disable Endpoint Security Agents
items.description: A standard non-admin account is sufficient to conduct an attack that exploits legitimate OS behavior rather than software vulnerabilities. The post macOS Weaknesses Chained to Silently Disable Endpoint Security Agents appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Indian auto giant Bajaj Auto hit by ransomware incident
items.description: The company said in a regulatory filing that it became aware of the incident on Tuesday morning and had taken precautionary measures to contain its impact.

items.title: Third DraftKings Hacker Sentenced to 18 Months in Prison
items.description: Nathan Austad has been ordered to pay roughly $1.8 million in forfeiture and restitution, and the sentence also includes 3 years of supervised release. The post Third DraftKings Hacker Sentenced to 18 Months in Prison appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Cordyceps CI/CD Flaws Expose 300+ GitHub Repositories to Supply-Chain Attacks
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new class of CI/CD workflow weakness that allows attackers to hijack workflows and compromise open-source supply chains. The "critical exploitable pattern" has been codenamed Cordyceps by Novee Security. The issue can allow full attacker control of repositories at dozens of the largest organizations worldwide, including Microsoft, Google, Apache, and

items.title: Critical Ubiquiti Vulnerabilities in Attackers’ Crosshairs
items.description: The flaws allow remote, unauthenticated attackers to make system changes, access underlying accounts, and inject commands. The post Critical Ubiquiti Vulnerabilities in Attackers’ Crosshairs appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Apple's MacOS Gap Lets Users Disable Security Tools
items.description: Attackers can exploit the issue to disable security and integrated browser tools without needing administrator privileges or kernel exploits.

items.title: Agentic AI Security: Wrong Context, Wrong Decisions at Machine Speed
items.description: Context is the central plank of AI in general, and agentic AI in particular. If an AI system doesn’t have the correct context, it cannot make the correct decisions. The post Agentic AI Security: Wrong Context, Wrong Decisions at Machine Speed appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: New ‘Mistic’ RAT Opens Door to Several Ransomware Families
items.description: Mistic is used by Woodgnat, an initial access broker working with Qilin, Interlock, Rhysida, Akira, 8Base, and Black Basta. The post New ‘Mistic’ RAT Opens Door to Several Ransomware Families appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Dawn of the Apex Agentic Adversary
items.description: We are standing at the end of an era we never thought to mourn: the era of human-speed threats. For years, cybersecurity moved to a rhythm organizations could follow. A researcher found a bug, a CVE was cataloged, a vendor navigated a patch cycle, and weeks or even months later, a fix was deployed. In this era, dwell time was measured in days, sometimes weeks. items.description: The security defects allow unauthenticated users to take control of the open source software supply chain. The post Exploitable CI/CD Vulnerabilities Expose Millions of Repositories to Hijacking appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Webinar Today: Modern Exposure Validation in the AI Era
items.description: The exploit timeline collapsed. Make sure your validation didn't. The post Webinar Today: Modern Exposure Validation in the AI Era appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Cisco Unified CM Flaw Exploited After PoC Reveals File-Write Path to Root
items.description: Threat actors have begun to exploit a recently disclosed critical security flaw impacting Cisco Unified Communications Manager (Unified CM) and Unified Communications Manager Session Management Edition (Unified CM SME). The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-20230 (CVSS score: 8.6), is a case of improper input validation for specific HTTP requests that could allow an unauthenticated, remote

items.title: Linux Process Name Masquerading, (Wed, Jun 24th)
items.description: In a previous diary, I talked about stack strings&&#x23;x26;&#x23;x5b;1&&#x23;x26;&#x23;x5d; with a practical example of them. Since my SEC670 class, I&&#x23;x26;&#x23;xe2;&&#x23;x26;&#x23;x80;&&#x23;x26;&#x23;x99;m even more interested&&#x23;x26;&#x23;xc2;&&#x23;x26;&#x23;xa0;in malware obfuscation techniques. I had&&#x23;x26;&#x23;xc2;&&#x23;x26;&#x23;xa0;a look at process names. When you list running processes on a computer, can you trust what you see&&#x23;x26;&#x23;x3f; If you&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;re facing a rootkit, malicious processes can be simply hidden (the API calls or commands to list processed have been tampered). But a malicious process&&#x23;x26;&#x23;xc2;&&#x23;x26;&#x23;xa0;can also mimic a non-suspicious name by

items.title: Cisco Unified CM flaw CVE-2026-20230 now exploited in attacks
items.description: A high-severity SSRF vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-20230, in Cisco Unified Communications Manager Server is now being exploited in attacks. [...]

items.title: Tata Electronics confirms cyberattack as hackers leak data
items.description: Tata Electronics has confirmed in a statement to BleepingComputer that it was the target of a cyberattack that impacted parts of its IT infrastructure. [...]

items.title: Scope of Salesforce Attacks Expands as Icarus Leaks Data
items.description: More victims have emerged after attackers breached application vendor Klue and used its OAuth tokens to steal customers' Salesforce data.

items.title: Windows 11 KB5095093 update rolls out new Point-in-Time restore feature
items.description: ​​Microsoft has released the KB5095093 preview cumulative update for Windows 11 24H2 and 25H2, which fixes numerous bugs and begins rolling out new features, including the new Point-in-Time restore feature. [...]

items.title: Healthtech firm Xolis suffers data breach impacting 1.4 million people
items.description: Healthcare technology company Xsolis says that sensitive data belonging to nearly 1.4 million individuals was compromised in a phishing attack that gave attackers access to its network. [...]

items.title: 'Cordyceps': Mushrooming Malicious Pull Requests Threaten Developer Workflows
items.description: The CI/CD workflow weakness affects Microsoft's Azure Sentinel, Google's AI Agent Development Kit, Apache's Doris analytics database, Cloudflare's Workers SDK, and Python Software Foundation's Black.

items.title: Five Eyes agencies sound alarm about AI’s threat to cybersecurity
items.description: "The timeline is not years, it is months,” the nations of the Five Eyes intelligence alliance said in a joint alert about the cybersecurity concerns of artificial intelligence.

items.title: New macOS ClickFix attack silently mounts DMGs to push infostealer
items.description: A new macOS ClickFix campaign is using Terminal commands to silently download, mount, and launch info-stealing malware from malicious disk image (DMG) files. [...]

items.title: FortiBleed Targeted FortiGate Firewalls in 110 Million-Credential Harvesting Operation
items.description: A Russian-speaking initial access broker (IAB) driven by financial gain is assessed to be behind a large-scale credential-harvesting operation known as FortiBleed that has targeted over 430,000 FortiGate firewalls globally. The campaign, active since February 2026, involves collecting credential lists, searching for exposed services, brute-forcing accessible systems, and deploying bespoke

items.title: Feds seize alleged cyber-scam infrastructure connected to Southeast Asian company
items.description: The Department of Justice announced the “seizure of a cloud computing account” used by subsidiaries of the Huione Group, a conglomerate severed from the U.S. financial system last year.

items.title: Trump directs federal agencies to protect US data from quantum threats
items.description: An executive order signed Monday aims to accelerate the government's transition to post-quantum cryptography (PQC), a new generation of encryption designed to protect data from the powerful quantum computers expected in the future.

items.title: Scattered Spider Hackers Plead Guilty on Day 1 of Trial
items.description: Two men pleaded guilty in the United Kingdom this week to criminal charges stemming from an August 2024 cyberattack that crippled Transport for London, the entity responsible for the public transport network in the Greater London area. The duo were key members of a prolific cybercrime group known as Scattered Spider, and their guilty pleas came on the first day of what was expected to be a six-week trial.

items.title: Scattered Spider members plead guilty to hacking Transport for London
items.description: Two members of the 'Scattered Spider' cybercrime group pleaded guilty to hacking the Transport for London (TfL) systems in 2024. [...]

items.title: Fake AI Agent Skill Passed Security Scans and Reportedly Reached 26,000 Agents
items.description: Security firm AIR built a fake AI agent skill, pushed it through a popular skill marketplace and an Instagram ad, and says it reached roughly 26,000 agents, including some on corporate accounts. Every skill security scanner the firm tested it against marked it safe. The payload was harmless by design: it collected the user's email address and did nothing else. The point was to show

items.title: Trump Order Sets 2030 Deadline for Federal Post-Quantum Crypto Migration
items.description: President Trump signed an executive order on June 22 setting hard deadlines for federal agencies to move high-value assets and high-impact systems to post-quantum cryptography. Key establishment must move by December 31, 2030; digital signatures by December 31, 2031. EO 14409 leaves national security systems on a separate track. The deadlines matter because of a threat that does not

items.title: GitHub Updates actions/checkout to Block Common Pwn Request Attack Patterns
items.description: GitHub is moving to strengthen software supply chain security by updating "actions/checkout" to block pwn request attacks that exploit the risky use of the "pull_request_target workflow" trigger to run malicious code with the workflow's full privileges. Effective June 18, 2026, the latest version of "actions/checkout," the official GitHub action for checking out a repository into the

items.title: SocGholish Takedown Highlights Malicious TDS Threats
items.description: SocGholish uses traffic distribution systems (TDSs) to provide initial access into victims' networks for cybercrime groups such as the notorious Evil Corp.

items.title: FortiBleed Attackers Turn Firewalls Into Credential Stealers as Heists Persist
items.description: The threat actors engineered a Golang-based sniffer to target 430,000 FortiGate firewalls and identify 110 million credentials in the ongoing global campaign.

items.title: Agentic AI: The Weapon That No Longer Needs a Warrior
items.description: Every weapon begins as an extension of the hand that holds it. The spear lengthened the reach of the arm. The bow sent the point flying without the throw. The rifle placed a man's death a quarter mile beyond his sight, and the aircraft carried that death across oceans. At each turn, the distance between the warrior and the wound grew wider, and yet one thing never moved: a human chose the target

items.title: Malicious npm Packages Pose as PostCSS Tools to Deliver Windows RAT
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a set of malicious npm packages that are designed to deliver a Windows-based remote access trojan (RAT). The list of identified packages, is below - aes-decode-runner-pro (145 downloads) postcss-minify-selector (256 downloads) postcss-minify-selector-parser (615 downloads) All the packages were published over the past month by an npm user named

items.title: CVE-2024-40766: The Patch Fixed the Bug. Nobody Fixed the Configuration., (Tue, Jun 23rd)
items.description: The vulnerability

items.title: DifyTap Bugs Let Attackers 'Wiretap' AI Chat Histories
items.description: Four vulnerabilities allow attackers to exploit Dify, a platform for AI application building and management, to silently access and exfiltrate sensitive data.

items.title: Crypto Heist Fueled by Elaborate Fake Reputation-Boosting Campaign
items.description: Attackers are using multiple online channels — including GitHub, YouTube, and VirusTotal — to build an illusion of trust to spread a cross-platform clipboard hijacker.

items.title: He Thought He Was Secure; His Phone Number Was Stolen Anyway
items.description: Threat actors can easily steal one-time passwords sent by text when they conduct a SIM swap attack. This can lead to account takeovers, so users must layer up their security measures.

items.title: Webshells Remain Popular, (Mon, Jun 22nd)
items.description: Webshells have been popular for a long time. We already covered this topic across multiple diaries[1][2]. I spent some time to track them[3] and slighly paid less attention to them but today I found another one. It seems to be a new player (pushed on Github two months ago).

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Monday, June 22nd, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9980, (Mon, Jun 22nd)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: Stressors, AI Forcing Changes to Cybersecurity Teams
items.description: As threats proliferate and AI complicates cybersecurity, CISOs say the job is getting harder, but more companies still want cybersecurity expertise, if even on a part-time basis.

items.title: eBanking Phishing Delivered Through IPv4-Mapped IPv6 Address, (Fri, Jun 19th)
items.description: I detected an interesting phishing email this morning. It targets a major Belgian bank:

items.title: Novo Nordisk Breach Highlights Software Development Pipeline Risk
items.description: A leaked GitHub token underscores what most organizations get wrong: Treating secrets management as a tooling problem rather than an identity problem.

items.title: ‘Popa’ Botnet Linked to Publicly-Traded Israeli Firm
items.description: For the past four years, a sprawling Android-based botnet called Popa has forced millions of consumer TV boxes to relay Internet traffic linked to advertising fraud, account takeovers, and mass data-scraping efforts. This week, researchers from multiple security firms concluded that the Popa botnet is linked to NetNut, a "residential proxy" provider operated by the publicly-traded Israeli firm Alarum Technologies Ltd [NASDAQ: ALAR].

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Thursday, June 18th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9978, (Thu, Jun 18th)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: The Behavior of Coordinated SSH Brute Force Attacks over the last three months [Guest Diary], (Wed, Jun 17th)
items.description: [This is a Guest Diary by Adam Nason, an ISC intern as part of the SANS.edu BACS program]

items.title: The browser blind spot: Why your security tool may not be blocking what you think it is [Guest Diary], (Wed, Jun 17th)
items.description: [This is a guest diary submitted by Varun Murdula]

items.title: Who Runs the Ransomware Group ‘The Gentlemen?’
items.description: A cybercrime group known as The Gentlemen has emerged as the second most active ransomware gang by victim count, rapidly attracting a talented pool of hackers through an aggressive recruitment strategy that promises affiliates 90 percent of any ransom paid by victims. This post examines clues pointing to a real life identity for the administrator of The Gentlemen ransomware group.

items.title: A Record-Breaking Patch Tuesday for June 2026
items.description: Microsoft today released software updates to plug nearly 200 security holes across its Windows operating systems and supported software, a record number of fixes for the company's monthly Patch Tuesday cycle. Nearly three dozen of those bugs earned Microsoft's most dire "critical" rating, and exploit code for at least three of the weaknesses is now publicly available.

items.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
items.description: The Instagram accounts for the Obama White House and the Chief Master Sergeant of the U.S. Space Force were briefly defaced with pro-Iranian images and messages over the weekend, after instructions began circulating on Telegram showing how to trick Meta's "AI support assistant" bot into resetting account passwords.

items.title: Netherlands Seizes 800 Servers, Arrests 2 for Aiding Cyberattacks
items.description: Authorities in the Netherlands have arrested the co-owners of two related Internet hosting companies for operating IT infrastructure used by Russia to carry out cyberattacks, influence operations and disinformation campaigns inside the European Union. The two men were the focus of a 2025 KrebsOnSecurity story about how their hosting companies had assumed control over the technical infrastructure of Stark Industries Solutions, an Internet service provider sanctioned last year by the EU as a frequent staging ground for cyber mischief from Russia's intelligence agencies.

items.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
items.description: Lawmakers in both houses of Congress are demanding answers from the U.S. Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) after KrebsOnSecurity reported this week that a CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and a vast trove of other agency secrets on a public GitHub account. The inquiry comes as CISA is still struggling to contain the breach and invalidate the leaked credentials.

items.title: Alleged Kimwolf Botmaster ‘Dort’ Arrested, Charged in U.S. and Canada
items.description: Canadian authorities on Wednesday arrested a 23-year-old Ottawa man on suspicion of building and operating Kimwolf, a fast spreading Internet-of-Things botnet that enslaved millions of devices for use in a series of massive distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks over the past six months. KrebsOnSecurity publicly named the suspect in February 2026 after the accused launched a volley of DDoS, doxing and swatting campaigns against this author and a security researcher. He now faces criminal hacking charges in both Canada and the United States.

items.title: CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
items.description: Until this past weekend, a contractor for the Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) maintained a public GitHub repository that exposed credentials to several highly privileged AWS GovCloud accounts and a large number of internal CISA systems. Security experts said the public archive included files detailing how CISA builds, tests and deploys software internally, and that it represents one of the most egregious government data leaks in recent history.

items.title: Patch Tuesday, May 2026 Edition
items.description: Artificial intelligence platforms may be just as susceptible to social engineering as human beings, but they are proving remarkably good at finding security vulnerabilities in human-made computer code. That reality is on full display this month with some of the more widely-used software makers -- including Apple, Google, Microsoft, Mozilla and Oracle -- fixing near record volumes of security bugs, and/or quickening the tempo of their patch releases.

items.title: Student Loan Breach Exposes 2.5M Records
items.description: 2.5 million people were affected, in a breach that could spell more trouble down the line.

items.title: Watering Hole Attacks Push ScanBox Keylogger
items.description: Researchers uncover a watering hole attack likely carried out by APT TA423, which attempts to plant the ScanBox JavaScript-based reconnaissance tool.

items.title: Tentacles of ‘0ktapus’ Threat Group Victimize 130 Firms
items.description: Over 130 companies tangled in sprawling phishing campaign that spoofed a multi-factor authentication system.

items.title: Ransomware Attacks are on the Rise
items.description: Lockbit is by far this summer’s most prolific ransomware group, trailed by two offshoots of the Conti group.

items.title: Cybercriminals Are Selling Access to Chinese Surveillance Cameras
items.description: Tens of thousands of cameras have failed to patch a critical, 11-month-old CVE, leaving thousands of organizations exposed.

items.title: Twitter Whistleblower Complaint: The TL;DR Version
items.description: Twitter is blasted for security and privacy lapses by the company’s former head of security who alleges the social media giant’s actions amount to a national security risk.

items.title: Firewall Bug Under Active Attack Triggers CISA Warning
items.description: CISA is warning that Palo Alto Networks’ PAN-OS is under active attack and needs to be patched ASAP.

items.title: Fake Reservation Links Prey on Weary Travelers
items.description: Fake travel reservations are exacting more pain from the travel weary, already dealing with the misery of canceled flights and overbooked hotels.

items.title: iPhone Users Urged to Update to Patch 2 Zero-Days
items.description: Separate fixes to macOS and iOS patch respective flaws in the kernel and WebKit that can allow threat actors to take over devices and are under attack.

items.title: Google Patches Chrome’s Fifth Zero-Day of the Year
items.description: An insufficient validation input flaw, one of 11 patched in an update this week, could allow for arbitrary code execution and is under active attack.
